verification.reasoning: User-controlled charset parameter from query string flows directly to HTTP Content-Type header without any CRLF sanitization, enabling HTTP response splitting attacks
verification.data_flow: query string extraction (line 25) → params dictionary creation (lines 25-26) → params.get("charset", "utf8") (line 78) → Content-Type header (line 78)
answers: Line 78: self.send_header("Content-Type", "%s%s" % ("text/html" if content.startswith("<!DOCTYPE html>") else "text/plain", "; charset=%s" % params.get("charset", "utf8")))
The value is constructed directly on line 78 using params.get("charset", "utf8")
The value derives from user input via the query string parsing that creates the params dictionary (lines 25-26)
No conditions override the charset parameter
No defense - there is no CRLF sanitization visible in the code path
User-controlled charset parameter from query string flows directly to Content-Type header without sanitization
